contents: R E V I E W A R T I C L EEffectiveness and safety of polidocanol for the treatment ofhemangiomas and vascular malformations: A meta-analysisZhi Gao|Yi Zhang|Wei Li|Chen ShiPharmacy Department, Plastic SurgeryHospital (Institute), Chinese Academy ofMedical Sciences and Peking Union MedicalCollege, Shijingshan District, Beijing 100144,ChinaCorrespondenceYi Zhang, Pharmacy Department, PlasticSurgery Hospital (Institute), ChineseAcademy of Medical Sciences and PekingUnion Medical College, No. 33 Badachu Rd,Shijingshan District, Beijing 100144, China.Email: AbstractA meta-analysis was conducted to investigate the efficacy and safety of polidocanol versus otherconventional therapies in treating hemangiomas (HMs) and vascular malformations (VMs).Literature search was conducted in the Cochrane, Embase, PubMed, Web of Science, CNKI, CBM,VIP, and WanFang databases until March 5, 2017. A meta-analysis was conducted using Revman5.3 software. A total of 19 randomized controlled trials (RCTs) involving 1,514 participants met theinclusion criteria. Regarding the effectiveness, statistically significant differences were observedbetween polidocanol and all the independent treatments (p 5 .006), but not between polidocanoland pingyangmycin (p 5 .16). Combination therapy of polidocanol with any other conventionaltreatments (p 5 .0001), pingyangmycin (p 5 .005) or hemoclip (p 5 .008) elicited a better responsecompared to treatment with these treatments independently. A meta-analysis on the risk of

adverse events (AEs) showed a lower risk for polidocanol versus other treatments, for example, allthe conventional treatments used independently (p < .00001) and pingyangmycin (p < .00001).Combination therapy of polidocanol with pingyangmycin also yielded a significantly lower risk ofAEs (p < .00001). Polidocanol is at least as effective as other conventional therapies on HMs andVMs (especially venous malformations). The former is much safer. Combining its use with othertreatments may produce excellent results. Our study provides strong evidence supporting the useof polidocanol for HMs and VMs.K E Y W O R D Shemangiomas, polidocanol, vascular malformations1| INTRODUCTIONHemangiomas (HMs) are vascular neoplasms or vascular anomalies dueto the proliferation of blood vessels, and vascular malformations (VMs)are the result of the arrested development of the venous system duringembryogenesis (Correa et al., 2007; Lee et al., 2015). The lesions com-monly involve the head and neck region followed by the trunk andextremities (Zheng et al., 2013). Most HMs disappear without treatment,

leaving minimal or no visible marks. However, when HMs interfere withvision or breathing or carry the threat of significant cosmetic injury(facial lesions and, in particular, nose and lip lesions), they are usuallytreated. Until recently, the mainstay of treatments include the system-atic use of corticosteroids (Nieuwenhuis, de Laat, Janmohamed, Madern,&Oranje, 2013) and beta-blockers (Petrovic et al., 2017), theintralesional injection of corticosteroid (Chantharatanapiboon, 2008;Prasetyono & Djoenaedi, 2011) and antitumor drugs (Hou, Wang, Tang,Wang, & Huang, 2011; Wang et al., 2009), the topical use of beta-blockers (Mashiah et al., 2017; Schneider, Reimer, Cremer, & Ruef,2014; Wang et al., 2017) and 5% imiquimod (Ho, Lansang, & Pope,2007; Ma et al., 2011; Welsh, Olazaran, Gomez, Salas, & Berman, 2004),and lasers (Li, Hu, Li, & Deng, 2016; Ying et al., 2017). Surgical removalis sometimes indicated, particularly if there has been delay in commenc-ing treatment and if structural changes have become irreversible(Essawy & Galal, 2013). It has been reported that sclerotherapy with

ethanol (Muto, Kinehara, Takahara, & Sato, 1990; Szejnfeld et al., 2015),sodium tetradecyl sulfate (Damodaran, McAuliffe, Wong, McCloskey, &Lee, 2012), and polidocanol (lauromacrogol 400; Winter, Drager, &Sterry, 2000) is an easy, safe, and effective modality for localized lesions.The combination use of sclerosants with other treatments has also beenreported (Grover, Arora, Kedar, Pal, & Lal, 2010); these treatments aimto reduce the duration and cumulative dose of both agents and aim touse their respective salient beneficial effects to our maximum benefit.Dermatologic Therapy. 2017;e12568. 2017 Wiley Periodicals, Inc. |1 of 8Received: 20 July 2017|Revised: 28 September 2017 |Accepted: 2 October 2017According to the modified Hamburg Classification of congenitalVMs, VMs can be classified into arterial malformations, venousmalformations, arterio-venous malformations, lymphatic malforma-tions, capillary malformations, and combined VMs (Lee et al., 2015), inwhich venous malformations are most common. Venous malforma-tions in the head and neck can often result in surface malformations

and organ translocation. Some lesions in the limbs and trunk may leadto the formation of thrombus, pain, and dysfunction because of theslow bloodstream. Thus, painful lesions and those on the surfaceshould be treated as early as possible. In the treatment of venousmalformations, the standard of care is a multidisciplinary approachincluding the use of traditional surgical therapy, if applicable, andendovascular treatments (Lee, Laredo, Kim, & Neville, 2007). Endo-vascular techniques include embolization therapy, for instance, withcoils but also sclerotherapy with liquid or foamed sclerosants. Foamsclerotherapy is recommended over liquid therapy for the treatmentof venous malformations (Rabe et al., 2014). Polidocanol is widelyreported to be a therapy for venous malformations (Blaise et al.,2011; Yamaki et al., 2008).Both HMs and VMs are congenital anomalies of the vascular sys-tem and have similar clinical manifestations. Therefore, their clinicalcases are often misdiagnosed. Sclerosants can be used in both cases.Polidocanol offers several advantages, such as having a local anestheticeffect, hence causing less pain during intralesional injection (Winteret al., 2000). However, few studies have systematically reviewed theadvantages of polidocanol in effectiveness and safety compared toother conventional therapies in treating the above lesions. In the pres-ent study, we performed a meta-analysis of the existing randomizedcontrolled trials (RCTs) to better elucidate the efficacy and safety ofpolidocanol in the treatment of HMs and VMs.2| MATERIAL AND METHODS2.1 | Literature search strategyComputer-based retrieval technology was applied to the electronicdatabases. We searched the Cochrane, Embase, PubMed, Web ofScience, China National Knowledge Infrastructure (CNKI), ChineseBiomedical Literature (CBM), VIP, and Wan Fang databases for allliterature from the database's date of launch up to March 5, 2017. Theretrieval of subject terms and free text was used. For PubMed, the

following search terms were used: "(polidocanol [supplementaryconcept] OR lauromacrogol OR polidocanol OR 'polyoxyethylene laurelether' OR aethoxysklerol) AND (Hemangioma[mh] OR 'vascular malfor-mation'[mh] OR Hemangiom* OR angiom* OR malformatio*)."2.2 | Inclusion criteriaWe included only RCTs involving patients with HMs or VMs. Trialswere eligible if they evaluated polidocanol as the intervention of inter-est in adults or children and compared it to other conventional thera-pies. The language was restricted to English and Chinese. In addition,only published studies were taken into consideration.2.3 | Study selection and data extractionTwo investigators (Zhi Gao and Yi Zhang) independently screened thesearch results, extracted the data using predefined forms and evaluatedthe risk of bias; all disputes were resolved by consensus. Study selec-tion was based on the title and abstract, and, if necessary, full-textarticles. The following data were extracted from studies that met the

inclusion criteria: characteristics of participants, intervention and regi-men details, clinical endpoints, and follow-up period. For the analysis ofefficacy, data on the reduced scale of the lesions were extracted. Thesafety evaluation included the presence of any adverse events (AEs).The methodological quality was assessed using the Cochrane Collabo-ration tool for the evaluation of risk of bias, which covers sequencegeneration, allocation concealment, blinding, incomplete data, selectivereporting, and other biases. In assessing the risk of bias, "1" denotescases of low risk of bias, "-" indicates a high risk of bias, whereas "?"indicates an unclear risk of bias.2.4 | Data synthesis and analysisRevman 5.3 software was used to perform the meta-analysis. For thesedichotomous outcomes, odds ratios (ORs) and 95% confidence inter-vals were estimated. In the present meta-analysis, either a random-effects model or a fixed-effects model (Mantel-Haenszel method) wasused for analysis. Heterogeneity among the studies was evaluated

using I2 statistics. I2 values of above 50% were defined as high hetero-geneity. Funnel plot asymmetry was measured to assess publicationbias. Probability values of < .05 were considered as statisticallysignificant.3| RESULTS3.1 | Literature search, description, and quality of theincluded studiesA total of 1,717 potentially relevant studies were identified in the liter-ature search, and 474 of them were duplicates. Then, 1,146 studieswere excluded after screening the titles and abstracts, and 78 studieswere excluded after screening the full-text. The remaining 19 RCTswere considered in the meta-analysis. The quality of the included trialswas relatively low. All trials were described as RCTs, but only threestudies reported a specific method of randomized allocation. Theothers only mentioned randomization. Furthermore, none of thearticles mentioned allocation concealment and blinding. Participantlosses were reported in two trials: two participants in one trial were

lost to follow-up and other unclear reasons, and four participants in theother trial were lost because of unclear reasons. These six patientswere excluded from the analysis.3.2 | Characteristics of the included studiesIncluded trials were published between 2011 and 2017. These trialsrandomized a total of 1,514 participants and possessed a sample sizeranging from 46 to 120 subjects. All of the trials were from China andTABLE 1Characteristics of the RCTs included in the meta-analysis1 (Bao, Zhao, Chen,Li, & Jiang, 2015)33/34(44.7 6 9.7 y)Liver HMsPL vs. PY30 mg/time (F)8-18622/248/21> 75% (If the criterionis > 50%, the effective ratebecomes 28/33:30/34)2 (Deng, 2015)34/34(11.3 6 3.6 y)Oromaxillo-facial HMsPL vs. PYNR (F)8432/305/21No criteria related to reducedscale5 (Tang, 2017)37 (28.9 6 6.5 y)/37(28.1 6 7.1 y)Head, neck and extremityVMsPL vs. PYNR (F)8NR35/277/17> 25% (If the criterionis > 50%, the effective ratebecomes 29/37:21/37)6 (Xu et al., 2015)54/52 (2 lost)(3 d-10 y)Oromaxillo-facial HMsPL vs. PY10 mg/cm2 (L)1-53-1254/4526/37> 2/38 (Zhang, Y. 11 (Feng, 2013)56/56(52.6 6 2.0 y)Oromaxillo-facial HMsPL vs. PR<10 mg/cm3 (L)31NR54/41N> 50%14 (Liu, Liu, Zhao,Cai, & Zhong,2016)60/60(16-52 y)Oromaxillo-facial andextremities VMsPL1PY vs. PY80-100 mg/site (L)8NR58/52109/58> 50%(Continues)were single-center studies. The basic characteristics of the studies areshown in Table 1. Of these included trials, 10 trials compared the effi-cacy of polidocanol with intralesional pingyangmycin, two comparedthe efficacy of polidocanol with intralesional ethanol, one trial com-pared the efficacy of polidocanol with oral prednisone, four trials com-pared the efficacy of a combination of intralesional polidocanol andpingyangmycin with pingyangmycin used independently, and two com-pared the efficacy of a combination of polidocanol and hemoclip withhemoclip used independently. Patients in 15 trials suffered from super-ficial HMs or VMs, such as those on the face, limbs, and abdomen, andpatients in four trials suffered from deep sites, such as the digestivetract or liver. Among these included trials, 12 trials included HM cases,

seven included VM cases in which five were venous malformations,and the remaining two were not described in detail. Three trials did notinclude statistical information regarding AEs.3.3 | Efficacy rateThe efficacy rate was reported in all of the included studies. In addition,in most of the references, efficacy was defined as a reduced scale of>50%. There was low heterogeneity in all the statistical analyses(I2 < 50%), and therefore, a fixed-effect model was used to evaluatethe data. As shown in Figure 1, two trials showed the same number ofeffective events in the experimental and control groups, and thus, theORs were not estimable. Statistically significant differences wereobserved between polidocanol and all of the drugs when used inde-pendently (p 5 .006) but not between polidocanol and pingyangmycin(p 5 .16). Combination therapy of polidocanol with any other conven-tional treatments (p 5 .0001), pingyangmycin (p 5 .005), or hemoclip(p 5 .008) elicited a better response compared to treatment with these

drugs independently (Figure 1).3.4 | Adverse eventsAdverse reactions were reported in 16 articles, including ulceration,swelling, pain, infection, necrosis of skin, gastrointestinal reaction, liverdamage, fever, dizziness, and amyotrophy, in either the experimentalgroup or the control group. The top three AEs are fever, swelling, andulceration in the experimental group and fever, swelling, and gastroin-testinal reaction in the control group. Three trials did not include statis-tical information regarding AEs and were excluded. There was lowheterogeneity in all the statistical analyses (I2 < 50%), and thus, a fixed-effect model was utilized. As shown in Figure 2, the AEs were countedby the number of occurrences in two trials, causing the number of AEsto be larger than that of total patients, and thus, the ORs were not esti-mable. A meta-analysis on the risk of AEs related to the treatmentsshowed a significantly lower risk for polidocanol versus other treat-ments, for example, all treatments used independently (p < .00001) and

pingyangmycin (p < .00001). Combination therapy of polidocanol withpingyangmycinalsoyieldedasignificantlylowerriskofAEs(p < .00001; Figure 2).TABLE 1(Continued)StudySamplesize (age)LocationInterventionDose of PL(liquid or foam)Duration(week)Follow-up(month)No. of efficacy(E/C)No. ofAE(E/C)Criteria for effectiveness(the reduced scale of thelesion area)16 (Zhang, H. This might be a result of the smaller sample sizes pro-ducing a smaller effect size.4| DISCUSSION

Our analysis of the 19 studies demonstrated that polidocanol was atleast as effective as other conventional therapies on HMs and VMs(especially venous malformations). In addition, the use of polidocanolwas much safer. The combination of polidocanol with other therapieswas also more effective and safer than treatment with those therapiesindependently.Sclerosants cause irreversible damage to the vascular endotheliumby disrupting cell membranes, resulting in sustained vasospasm and thedenudation of the venous monolayer (Bergan, 2008). Sclerotherapy forHMs and VMs has been reported. Polidocanol, a sclerosant, offers sev-eral advantages, such as the local anesthetic effect, hence causing lesspain during the injection (Winter et al., 2000). Despite the growingacceptance of polidocanol as a leading treatment modality in HMs andVMs, few studies have systematically reviewed the advantages of poli-docanol compared to other common treatments. In the present study,we performed a meta-analysis of the existing RCTs to better elucidate

the efficacy and safety of polidocanol in the treatment of HMs andVMs. The strengths of this meta-analysis are that it compared the useof polidocanol with the use of other therapies and also simultaneouslyanalyzed the superior efficacy of a combination treatment.However, there are several limitations that should be addressed.First, the quality of the included trials was relatively low. All trials weredescribed as RCTs, but only three studies reported a specific methodof randomized allocation, the others only mentioned randomization.Furthermore, none of the articles mentioned allocation concealmentand blinding. Participant losses were reported in two trials: twoFIGURE 2Comparison of the risk of adverse events from polidocanol use with that from the use of other conventional therapies in thetreatment of hemangiomas and vascular malformationsparticipants in one trial were lost to follow-up and other unclear rea-sons, and four participants in the other trial were lost because ofunclear reasons. Unreasonable losses may influence outcome. The anal-ysis of additional high-quality studies will be required. Second, of these19 articles, 18 were published in Chinese, and 1 was in English. All of

the participants were from China, resulting in geographical limitations.Third, the outcome measurement varied across the studies, whichweakened the strength of the identified associations. Several studiesused different percentages to determine the reduced scale of thelesions, whereas others used objective methods such as doppler ultra-sonography to evaluate the responses. This discrepancy may lead toinevitable bias. Fourth, different forms of polidocanol were used, whichmay influence the efficacy. Many studies have shown that foam polido-canol has the advantage of better and longer lasting contact of thesclerosing agent with the vessel wall, which leads to a more effectivetreatment and a reduction of the necessary drug concentration (Rabe &Pannier, 2013).Based on the findings of the present analysis, a few questionsremain to be answered. First, if necessary and if there are enough data,the heterogeneity in the age of the patients as well as the type and thelocation of the lesions should be taken into account. Second, due to

the lack of dose-response studies, the optimal dose of polidocanolremains unknown. To answer these questions, additional well-designedRCT studies need to be conducted.In conclusion, polidocanol is an effective and safe therapy for HMsand VMs (especially venous malformations). In addition, combinationtherapy of polidocanol with other treatments will produce excellentresponses, with minimal AEs. The present meta-analysis providesstrong evidence supporting the use of polidocanol for the treatment ofHMs and VMs.CONFLICTS OF INTERESTThe authors declare that they have no competing interests.ORCIDREFERENCESBao, H.
